status_notes: Policy A1 assessment logic is intended for householder planning permission and pre-application advice on equivalent proposals.
It is not the decision rule pack for larger home extension prior notification. That route is distinct and has its own neighbour-consultation style assessment.

National/local validation content combines live Gloucester submission guidance, adopted policy context and some provisional draft-2026 local validation material. Consumers must preserve source status when reporting outcomes.
Predicate field names in applicability conditions map to the facts schema via the predicate_mapping table in the facts schema.
v4.3 adds machine-readable legal_basis and effect_type fields to validation requirements and assessment rules.
v4.3 adds threshold_status for rules that rely on locally-applied thresholds without an adopted published source.

verified_sources.title: Submitting a planning application
verified_sources.notes: Confirms invalid application process and that website still points applicants to current checklist plus October 2023 addendum.

verified_sources.title: Local Validation Checklist Public Consultation
verified_sources.notes: Confirms revised 2026 LVC was placed on public consultation, not yet adopted.

verified_sources.title: Draft Local Validation Checklist January 2026
verified_sources.notes: Provides draft validation content and appendices used here as provisional local validation rules.

verified_sources.title: Community Infrastructure Levy (CIL)
verified_sources.notes: Confirms extension/annexe threshold and relief availability.

verified_sources.title: Biodiversity net gain: exempt developments
verified_sources.notes: Used to mark householder applications as exempt from mandatory BNG.

decision_semantics.must: Mandatory requirement or strong legal/policy constraint.
decision_semantics.must_not: Outcome should be treated as non-compliant unless strong contrary basis exists.
decision_semantics.should: Normal policy expectation; departures need justification and officer judgement.
decision_semantics.should_not: Normally unacceptable; departures need strong justification and officer judgement.
decision_semantics.may: Potentially acceptable factor requiring context-based judgement.
decision_semantics.informative_only: Not a decision rule; information or good practice only.
decision_semantics.manual_review_required: Automation should stop and request human review.

route_notes: Prior notification for larger home extensions is deliberately separated because it is not a normal planning-permission merits exercise.
Where listed building consent is also needed, Policy A1 does not displace listed-building statutory duties; it runs alongside a heritage review.

national_requirements._description: Statutory national requirements. Applications without any one of these are likely to be invalidated.

A1_completed_application_form.name: Completed Application Form
A1_completed_application_form.applies_to: All applications for planning permission and associated consents
A1_completed_application_form.validation_rule: All sections of the relevant application form must be completed

A2_ownership_certificate.name: Ownership Certificate and Agricultural Land Declaration

A.when: Applicant has sole ownership and no agricultural tenants

B.when: Applicant is not sole owner, or there are agricultural tenants, but names/addresses of all others are known

C.when: Applicant does not own all land and does not know all owners/agricultural tenants
additional_requirements: Submit copy of published notice with application

D.when: Applicant does not own all land and knows none of the owners/agricultural tenants
additional_requirements: Submit copy of published notice with application

A3_planning_application_fee.name: Planning Application Fee
A3_planning_application_fee.policy_driver: Part 3, Article 11, DMPO 2015 (as amended); Town and Country Planning (Fees) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2023
A3_planning_application_fee.applies_to: Most applications (concessions exist for LBC, conservation area demolition, tree works)
A3_planning_application_fee.exemption_rule: If seeking exemption, reasons must be clearly stated in submission

A4_biodiversity_net_gain_strategy.name: Biodiversity Net Gain Strategy
A4_biodiversity_net_gain_strategy.policy_driver: Schedule 7A, TCPA 1990 (as inserted by Schedule 14, Environment Act 2021)
A4_biodiversity_net_gain_strategy.notes: Householder applications are exempt from BNG Strategy requirement
A4_biodiversity_net_gain_strategy.scope_note: Mandatory statutory BNG does not apply to householder applications. Do not treat this item as a required householder validation document.

A5_environmental_impact_statement.name: Environmental Impact Statement
A5_environmental_impact_statement.policy_driver: Town and Country Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations 2017
A5_environmental_impact_statement.notes: Not applicable to householder applications in normal circumstances

A6_fire_statement.name: Fire Statement
A6_fire_statement.trigger: Buildings 18m+ height or 7+ storeys with 2+ dwellings or educational accommodation
A6_fire_statement.notes: Not applicable to standard householder applications

A7_design_and_access_statement.name: Design and Access Statement
A7_design_and_access_statement.policy_driver: Part 2, Article 9, DMPO 2015 (as amended); Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) Regulations 1990; NPPF 137-139, 167

standard: Explain design principles and concepts applied to the proposed development
Demonstrate steps taken to appraise context and how design takes context into account
listed_building: Explanation of design principles applied to the proposed works
How they have taken account of the special architectural or historic importance of the building
How they have taken account of the particular physical features justifying the listing
How they have taken account of the building's setting

A7_design_and_access_statement.scope_note: For this ruleset, DAS is relevant to planning-permission routes in designated areas and to listed-building consent. It is not a generic householder requirement.

A8_site_location_plan.name: Site Location Plan
A8_site_location_plan.applies_to: All applications except S73 variation/discharge of condition and non-material amendment

required_content: Sufficient roads (usually 2 road names) and/or buildings on adjoining land to identify exact location
Direction of North
Scale bar
Red line around application site boundary (including all land for access, visibility splays, landscaping, parking, open areas)
Blue line around any other land owned by applicant close to or adjoining the site

local_requirements_householder._description: Local validation requirements applicable to householder applications. These are conditional — triggered by site characteristics, proposal type, or location.

policy_tests.description: Functional link with the principal dwelling; accommodation is ancillary with reliance on connection with main dwelling
Subservient design and scale to the existing dwelling
Same ownership as the principal dwelling
No boundary demarcation or sub-division of garden areas between annexe and principal dwelling
Clear need to accommodate a relative, dependant or full-time carer

annexe_functional_link_table._description: Appendix 2 of the Local Validation Checklist. Must be completed and submitted prior to validation.

services.column_a: Contained in annexe itself or separate to/not shared with main dwelling?
services.column_b: Contained in or shared with main dwelling?

services.column_a: Contained in annexe itself or separate to/not shared with main dwelling?
services.column_b: Contained in or shared with main dwelling?

B9_bin_store.name: Bin Store

trigger.note: All proposals involving development that would require bin storage, including all new dwellings. For householder extensions this is generally relevant only where the extension affects existing bin storage arrangements.

required_content: Bin storage shown on block plan (separate plan for major schemes - not typical for householder)
Drawings should clearly show appropriate bin storage and identify collection points
Scale: 1:100, 1:200 or 1:500 depending on size/context
Statement confirming compliance with Gloucester City Council Waste and Recycling Guidance for New Residential Development
B9_bin_store.critical_note: Bins and recycling boxes cannot be collected by the local authority where a collection vehicle or member of collection staff is required to enter private property including stepping onto private drives

B11_biodiversity_small_sites_statement.name: Biodiversity Small Sites Statement
B11_biodiversity_small_sites_statement.required_content: Short statement outlining how the applicant will improve biodiversity
examples_of_measures: Artificial nests for birds (swift bricks or bird boxes)
notes: This item is a local validation expectation for biodiversity information. It is not the statutory mandatory Biodiversity Net Gain regime.

B12_biodiversity_survey_and_report.name: Biodiversity Survey and Report
required_content: Current habitat assessment and impact assessment
Effect on any protected species
Considerations and mitigation techniques
Summary of all recent surveys required with confirmation they have been undertaken at appropriate times of year

survey_validity.12_to_18_months: Valid provided site conditions have not changed

habitats_regulations_assessment.trigger: Application may be connected to or potentially impact European/Internationally designated site (Ramsar, SAC, SPA, or candidate status)

B16_condition_survey.name: Condition Survey
required_content: Fabric deterioration assessment
Materials decay assessment
Weathering assessment
Assessment of structural condition (where required)
Prognosis of likely effects of deterioration processes
Repair strategy or schedule of works to restore
B16_condition_survey.preparer: Appropriately qualified specialist familiar with heritage assets
B16_condition_survey.note: Also required for conversion of existing building to residential where significant alterations/repair/works are needed

B17_cordon_sanitaire.name: Cordon Sanitaire – Netheridge Sewage Treatment Works
B17_cordon_sanitaire.required_content: Odour Assessment with comprehensive detailed odour dispersion modelling, source monitoring and ground based observations
B17_cordon_sanitaire.standard: Institute of Air Quality Management (IAQM) guidance
B17_cordon_sanitaire._LOCK_TODO_002: AWAITING: No interactive policy map with Cordon Sanitaire boundary found online. Evidence study PDF at https://www.gloucester.gov.uk/media/xd1j01oe/8693-odour-final.pdf — boundary data may need GIS extract from Environmental Health or Planning Policy team.

B18_daylight_sunlight_assessment.name: Daylight/Sunlight Assessment

trigger.note: Includes impact on associated gardens or amenity space

required_content.scope_statement: Identify whether assessment covers impact on neighbouring properties, quality of light in proposed development, and amenity spaces
model_and_methodology: Modelling software details, assumptions and parameters
Existing and proposed buildings, ground levels, tree cover (leaf-on/off)
Surface reflectance and glass transmittance values
neighbouring_properties_analysis: Vertical Sky Component (VSC) results for affected windows
No-Sky Line (NSL) analysis for internal daylight distribution
Annual Probable Sunlight Hours (APSH) for main living rooms (annual and winter)
Sunlight to gardens/open spaces on 21 March
proposed_development_analysis: Internal daylight compliance using SDA, Median Daylight Factor, or lux levels per BS EN 17037
Internal sunlight hours for habitable rooms
results: Weighted averages for rooms with multiple windows
required_content.flexibility_and_justification: Statement explaining any deviations from BRE targets and contextual justification
required_content.executive_summary: Clear summary of compliance, impacts, and any mitigation measures

B18_daylight_sunlight_assessment.guidance_reference: Site Planning for Daylight and Sunlight: a guide to good practice. BRE Guidance

B20_drainage_strategy.name: Drainage Strategy Including Sustainable Urban Drainage (SUDS)
B20_drainage_strategy.also_required: Flood Risk & Drainage Validation Checklist (see flood_risk_drainage_checklist section)

householder_guidance_urls._LOCK_TODO_003: PARTIAL: Individual flood zone checklist PDFs are linked from the submitting page. No single householder-specific FRA guidance document found. Confirm with Planning team whether a standalone householder FRA guide exists or whether the checklists are the complete requirement.

required_content: Suitable consideration has been given to surface water drainage
Appropriate arrangements for attenuating surface water run-off as close as possible to its source can be accommodated within the site
Issues of ownership and maintenance are addressed

B25_flood_risk_assessment.name: Flood Risk Assessment

any_of: Site in Flood Zone 1 with area >= 1 hectare
Site in Flood Zones plus Climate Change showing increased risk
Site in Flood Zone 1 with high surface water flood risk
Site in area with critical drainage problems
Site in Flood Zone 1 where SFRA shows increased risk during lifetime
Development increases vulnerability classification and may be subject to flooding

sequential_test.required: For all development in flood risk areas except exemptions
exemptions_for_householder: Minor development for flood risk (householder development and small non-residential extensions with footprint < 250sqm)
sequential_test.note: Most householder applications are exempt from the sequential test as they are classified as minor development for flood risk purposes

required_when: Highly vulnerable development in Flood Zone 2
Essential infrastructure in Flood Zone 3a or 3b
More vulnerable development in Flood Zone 3a
criteria: Development provides wider sustainability benefits that outweigh flood risk
Development will be safe for its lifetime without increasing flood risk elsewhere, and where possible will reduce flood risk overall

B25_flood_risk_assessment.key_constraint: Only water compatible and essential infrastructure development is permitted in Flood Zone 3b
B25_flood_risk_assessment.climate_change_allowances: FRAs must incorporate the most up-to-date climate change allowances

B28_historic_impact_statement.name: Historic Impact Statement

any_of: Unlisted historic building or structure affected
Heritage asset identified during development preparation

required_content: Description of the significance of any heritage assets affected, including contribution made by their setting
Demonstration that historic character and distinctiveness of the locality has been assessed and taken into account
Consultation of the Historic Environment Record as a minimum
B28_historic_impact_statement.proportionality: Scope shall be proportionate to the importance of the heritage asset(s) and no more than sufficient to understand the impact
B28_historic_impact_statement.pre_application_advice: Applicants strongly advised to submit pre-application for guidance on extent of information required

B34_lighting_assessment.name: Lighting Assessment
required_content: Light spillage assessment
Hours of illumination
Light levels
Column heights
Specification and colour treatment for lamps and luminaries
Need for full horizontal cut off
No distraction to highway
Levels of impact on nearby dwellings
Use of demountable columns
Retention of screening vegetation
Use of planting and bunding to contain lighting effects
Details of lighting covers or cowling
Other mitigation measures
B34_lighting_assessment.standard: Institute of Lighting Professionals GN01 For the reduction of obtrusive light 2021
B34_lighting_assessment.note: Unlikely to be triggered for most householder applications but included for completeness

B37_nationally_described_space_standards.name: Nationally Described Space Standards Statement

trigger.note: Only applies to householder applications where an annexe constitutes a new dwelling - otherwise not triggered for extensions

required_content: Floor plans with all dimensions including internal floor space, storage areas, areas of reduced ceiling height
Table (preferably excel/csv) showing gross internal floorspace, room dimensions, and storage provision of each property
Whether or not it meets the NDSS standard and the amount by which it exceeds or falls short
Which NDSS criteria applied (e.g. 1b1p, 3b5p, 5b8p)

required_content.licensing_route_choice: State whether National England Licensing Route or District Licensing Route

B52_regulation_77_permitted_development.name: Regulation 77 Application for Permitted Development
policy_drivers: Conservation of Habitats and Species Regulations 2017 (as amended)

trigger.note: Development types deemed to have a Likely Significant Effect on a Special Area of Conservation (Cotswold Beechwoods)

required_content: Details of intended development
Optional: Copy of any relevant notification by appropriate nature conservation body under Regulation 76
Application Fee
Site location plan
Existing and proposed block/site plans, elevational drawings
Reference number of any Prior Approval application
Appropriate long or short form S106 Unilateral Undertaking legal agreement

B62_trees_survey_and_protection.name: Trees Survey and Tree Protection Plan

any_of: Proposals affecting trees covered by a Tree Preservation Order
Proposals within a Conservation Area where trees within or adjacent the site are affected
All sites with trees and hedges growing within or bounding them with stem diameter > 75mm at 1.5m above ground level where proposals affect them

required_content: Tree survey
Tree works method statement
Tree Protection Plan
B62_trees_survey_and_protection.standards: BS5837:2012 'Trees in relation to design, demolition and construction – recommendations' or subsequent revisions

content: Plan indicating trees for retention and removal
Evaluation of impact of proposed tree losses
Specialist issues addressable by arboricultural method statement

details_needed: Location, species, size and canopy volume
Measurement of new canopy volume compared to previous canopy volume (CAVAT value)

B62_trees_survey_and_protection.householder_simplified: For householder applications, any proposals involving development within 2m of the canopy spread of any tree(s) within or adjoining the site should indicate the position and spread of the tree(s) on the application plans

householder_plans_requirements._description: Drawing requirements for householder applications derived from Appendix 1 and Part B items 43-47 of the Local Validation Checklist.

existing_and_proposed_block_plan.name: Site/Block Plan Existing and Proposed
existing_plan_must_show: Footprint of all existing buildings on site with written dimensions and distances to site boundaries
proposed_plan_must_show: Footprint of proposed development and all buildings to be retained with written dimensions and distances to site boundaries
All buildings, roads and footpaths on adjoining land including access arrangements
All public rights of way crossing or adjoining the site
Position of all proposed trees and those retained on site, and those on adjacent land
Extent and type of hard surfacing and boundary treatment including type and height of walls or fencing

parking_requirements.trigger: Loss or gain in parking provision, increase in parking demand, or change to vehicular/pedestrian access
must_show: Parking arrangements including dimensions of spaces
Secure cycle parking (location and size)
Pedestrian access if appropriate
parking_requirements.note: If any spaces have smaller dimensions than adopted G1 policy sizes, justification must be provided

elevation_drawings.name: Elevation Drawings – Existing and Proposed
elevation_drawings.trigger: All applications proposing new buildings, extensions or alterations to the exterior of an existing building
elevation_drawings.quantity: Two sets required: one existing, one proposed
must_show: Where existing buildings or walls are to be demolished (if applicable)
Details of existing building(s) and proposed development
Written dimensions showing overall size of new buildings or extensions
Direction of North
Scale bar

floor_plans.name: Floor Plans – Existing and Proposed
floor_plans.trigger: All applications proposing new or amended floor space and/or proposals to alter existing buildings
floor_plans.quantity: Two sets required: one existing, one proposed
must_show: Where existing buildings or walls are to be demolished (if applicable)
Details of existing building(s) and proposed development
Written dimensions (width and depth) showing overall size of new buildings or extensions
Scale bar

site_sections_and_levels.name: Site Sections, Finished Floor and Site Levels – Existing and Proposed
site_sections_and_levels.trigger: Applications involving a change in ground levels or development next to changing ground levels
must_show: Existing and proposed finished ground levels
Details of floor levels, building height and relationship to site boundaries
On sloping sites: how proposals relate to existing ground levels where ground levels may be modified

roof_plans.name: Roof Plans – Existing and Proposed
roof_plans.trigger: Where a roof would be created or altered by the proposed development
must_show: Shape of the roof
Roofing materials
Features such as chimney positions or windows
roof_plans.note: May be possible to combine with proposed block plan where the roof plan is simple and clearly conveyed

flood_risk_drainage_checklist._description: Appendix 3 of the Local Validation Checklist. Required where the application needs a Flood Risk Assessment or Drainage Strategy.

H1_flood_risk_assessment.trigger: Development in Flood Zone 2 or 3 (taking into account climate change)

H1.1.description: Simple Flood Risk Assessment report following Government Flood Risk Standing Advice Guidance

H1.2.description: Identification of the flood zone within which the site is located
H1.2.data_source: Flood maps for planning (dataset: flood zones 2 and 3; climate change - 2070 to 2125)

H1.3.description: Up-to-date design flood level for the site
H1.3.specification: 100yr (or 1%) flood level with a 37% climate change allowance

H1.4.description: Finished floor level (FFL) related to Ordnance Datum (m AOD)
H1.4.rule: For extensions: floor level should be no lower than existing floor level, preferably no less than 300mm above design flood level

H1.5.description: External ground levels of the site to Ordnance Datum (m AOD)

H1.6.description: Brief assessment of flooding risk from other sources

H1.7.description: Details of flood resilience / flood resistance proposals
H1.7.specification: Required to a height of 600mm above the design flood level

H1.8.description: Flood Zone 3 Only: Floodplain storage volume compensation
H1.8.trigger: Development displaces more than 5 cubic metres of flood plain storage volume

H2_impermeable_area_increase.trigger: Increase in impermeable area (building footprint + hard surfacing) of 50 sqm or more

must_show: All proposed new roof / driveway / paving areas marked with sizes in sqm
Proposed drainage layout including location/size of flow control, surface water attenuation/storage, soakaways

H2.2.description: Where soakaways are proposed: BRE 365 infiltration testing results and soakaway sizing calculations

H3.1.description: Identify any areas within 8m of a watercourse (from top of bank), including culverted watercourses (8m from edge of culvert)
H3.1.constraint: No built development within 8m of watercourses (both main river and ordinary watercourses)

signoff.declaration: I confirm that I have included the required information and have accurately completed this checklist
understanding: Failure to include required information will result in delays to validation
If inaccuracies are found, the LPA will not make further requests for information and the application will be determined on submitted information

assessment_tests.description: Extensions should be clearly subordinate to the main dwelling and not dominate its appearance

rules.policy_rationale: Side extensions set back from the main front building line appear subordinate and avoid creating a 'terracing effect' on pairs or groups of dwellings
rules.assessment_guidance: Measure from the furthest forward point of the existing front elevation to the front face of the proposed side extension. A setback of 1m is the minimum expected; 1.5m is preferred for two-storey extensions on semi-detached dwellings.

exceptions.description: No setback required where existing pattern shows continuous front building line (e.g., terraced streets)
Reduced setback may be acceptable on detached dwellings with significant plot width

suggested_condition_templates: The development shall be carried out in accordance with the approved plans, which show the side extension set back [X]mm from the front building line of the main dwelling.

rules.policy_rationale: Extensions with ridge heights lower than or equal to the existing dwelling appear subordinate and respect the hierarchy of built form
rules.assessment_guidance: Compare the proposed ridge height with the existing main dwelling ridge. Two-storey side extensions should typically have a lower ridge. Matching ridge height may be acceptable if other subordination features are present (setback, reduced width, secondary materials).

exceptions.description: Matching ridge height acceptable if strong subordination achieved through other means

rules.policy_rationale: Matching or slightly lower eaves heights create visual continuity while maintaining overall subordination
rules.assessment_guidance: Eaves may match the existing dwelling where this creates better proportions and design coherence. This is particularly acceptable where ridge height is lower or other subordination features are present.

rules.policy_rationale: Extensions narrower than the main dwelling appear subordinate and maintain the character of the original building
rules.assessment_guidance: Calculate extension width as a percentage of the existing main dwelling width. Extensions should not overwhelm the proportions of the original building.

rules.policy_rationale: Front extensions should be modest additions that do not dominate the front elevation or harm street scene character
rules.assessment_guidance: Porches and front extensions should be clearly subordinate features. Single-storey depth of up to 1.5m is typically acceptable; deeper extensions require strong design justification.

assessment_tests.description: Extensions must not cause unacceptable harm to the amenities of neighbouring properties through loss of light, overlooking, or overbearing impact

rules.policy_rationale: The 45-degree rule is a standard test for ensuring extensions do not cause unacceptable loss of daylight to neighboring properties
rules.assessment_guidance: Identify the nearest habitable room window (living room, kitchen, bedroom - not bathroom or hallway) on the adjoining property. Plot a 45-degree line from its center point. Extensions breaching this line may cause unacceptable loss of light. Site-specific factors (orientation, existing obstructions, room usage) should be considered.

metric.description: Draw a 45-degree line from the center point of the nearest ground floor habitable room window of the adjoining property. The extension should not breach this line in elevation view.

exceptions.description: Breach may be acceptable if window serves a secondary room and other windows are unaffected
North-facing extensions have lower impact on light than south-facing

rules.policy_rationale: Adequate separation prevents mutual overlooking between habitable room windows
rules.assessment_guidance: Measure the distance between the proposed habitable room window and the nearest habitable room window on the facing property. 21m is the standard for directly facing windows; oblique views may allow reduced distances.

metric.note: 21m is a standard separation distance for rear-to-rear; side-to-side may be less depending on context

rules.policy_rationale: Windows in side elevations close to boundaries create potential for future overlooking, particularly if the neighbouring site is developed
rules.assessment_guidance: Side elevation windows serving habitable rooms should be avoided where they would overlook neighbouring properties. Where they are necessary, obscure glazing and non-opening below 1.7m conditions should be applied.
Consider whether new upper-floor windows would introduce materially greater overlooking of neighbouring gardens than the existing situation. Raised terraces and balconies require particular scrutiny.

metric.note: Half the standard 21m separation is typically applied to garden boundaries

rules.policy_rationale: Balconies, terraces and raised platforms can cause significant overlooking that is disproportionate to the scale of development
rules.assessment_guidance: Balconies and roof terraces on extensions are generally resisted unless they can be demonstrated not to cause unacceptable overlooking. Flat roofs on extensions should not be used as balconies or sitting-out areas without appropriate screening.

Consider the combined effect of height, length and proximity on the neighbouring property. A tall, long extension close to a boundary with a small neighbouring garden oriented to the north will have a significantly greater overbearing impact than a modest extension next to a large south-facing garden.

factors: Height of extension relative to boundary
Size and aspect of neighbouring garden

rules.policy_rationale: Adequate private amenity space must be retained for the occupiers of the dwelling
rules.assessment_guidance: Measure from the rear wall of the proposed extension to the rear boundary. Less than 10m should be carefully justified. The quality and usability of the remaining space matters as much as the raw measurement.

metric.note: 10m minimum remaining garden depth is a widely applied standard

authoring_locks._LOCK_TODO_008: AWAITING OFFICER CONFIRMATION: No published SPD or adopted standard for minimum garden depth found. The 10m figure appears to be common officer practice across similar authorities. Confirm with Development Management team whether this is formal policy, informal guidance, or case-by-case. If formal, cite the source document.

assessment_tests.description: Extensions should use appropriate materials and architectural details that complement the host dwelling

rules.policy_rationale: Matching materials ensure the extension appears as a coherent part of the dwelling rather than an incongruous addition

rules.assessment_guidance: Architectural details should either match the existing dwelling or provide a clearly contemporary but complementary approach. Poor-quality pastiche or obviously cheap imitation details should be avoided.

rules.policy_rationale: Roof forms and pitches that match or complement the existing dwelling create visual harmony
rules.assessment_guidance: Pitched roofs should generally match the pitch of the existing dwelling. Flat roofs on single-storey extensions are acceptable where they would not be visible from the public realm or where a pitched roof would create an unacceptable eaves height.

assessment_tests.description: Extensions must not harm the character and appearance of the street scene

rules.policy_rationale: Side extensions on semi-detached and end-terrace dwellings can create a 'terracing effect' that harms the character of the pair or row
rules.assessment_guidance: Consider whether the extension would close the visual gap between dwellings and create the appearance of a continuous terrace. Setback from front building line, lower ridge, and distinct materials/detailing can mitigate this effect.
Consider the extension's impact on the front elevation as seen from the street. Extensions that are clearly visible from the public realm require greater design care.

In streets with a strong, consistent character (e.g., estate developments, conservation areas, planned Victorian/Edwardian streets), extensions should be particularly sensitive to maintaining the rhythm and proportions of the streetscape.

assessment_tests.description: Extensions must maintain adequate parking provision and safe access

rules.assessment_guidance: Check that the extension does not result in the loss of required parking spaces. Where spaces are lost, consider whether the remaining provision meets the adopted standards.

parking_space_dimensions.source: GCC HMO Guide (September 2023) referencing Policy G1; confirmed minimum car parking space is 2.4m x 4.8m
parking_space_dimensions._LOCK_TODO_009: PARTIAL: Minimum space dimensions confirmed via HMO Guide. Full Policy G1 text in adopted City Plan PDF may contain additional requirements for parallel spaces, disabled bays, or garage internal dimensions. Confirm with Highways whether any supplementary parking standards document exists.

rules.assessment_guidance: Where the extension affects vehicle access or changes visibility at the access point, highway safety must be maintained.
Consider whether the extension creates additional bedrooms that would generate cycle parking demand. Secure cycle storage should be available.

authoring_locks._LOCK_TODO_010: AWAITING: No householder-specific cycle parking standard found. Policy G1 defers to Gloucestershire County Council guidance (Cycle Facility Guidelines March 2012, Manual for Streets 4th Ed April 2016). Unlikely to be triggered for standard householder extensions unless additional bedrooms are created.

assessment_tests.description: Extensions should address surface water drainage, biodiversity, and energy efficiency

rules.policy_rationale: Increased impermeable area can increase surface water runoff and flood risk
rules.assessment_guidance: Where the extension and associated hard surfacing increases the impermeable area by 50sqm or more, a drainage strategy is required. See validation requirement B20 and the Flood Risk & Drainage Checklist.

rules.policy_rationale: All developments are required to improve biodiversity
rules.assessment_guidance: A Biodiversity Small Sites Statement is a validation requirement for householder applications. Measures should be proportionate - e.g., integrated bird boxes, bat bricks, native planting.
notes: This rule addresses biodiversity enhancement in planning merits. It does not create or imply statutory mandatory BNG for householder applications, which are exempt.
note: No specific householder energy efficiency requirement in current policy but good practice

rules.assessment_guidance: Applicants are encouraged to incorporate energy efficiency measures. This is not a validation requirement for householder applications.

authoring_locks._LOCK_TODO_011: AWAITING: No current adopted energy efficiency requirement for householder applications. Check emerging JCS Review and any future SPD or Building Regulations alignment. This rule is informative-only until a policy requirement is adopted.

assessment_tests.description: Extensions affecting heritage assets must preserve or enhance their significance

rules.assessment_guidance: Extensions to listed buildings or within their setting require a Historic Impact Statement. The extension must preserve the special architectural or historic interest of the building. Listed building consent will be required for internal and external alterations.

Extensions in conservation areas must preserve or enhance the character or appearance of the area. Design and Access Statement required for development consisting of one or more dwellings or buildings with floor space of 100sqm or more.
rules.additional_requirement: A Design and Access Statement may be required - see A7

rules.assessment_guidance: Where the site has known or potential archaeological interest, a desk-based assessment or field evaluation may be required as part of the Historic Impact Statement.

assessment_tests.description: Extensions must be safe from flooding and must not increase flood risk elsewhere

rules.assessment_guidance: Development in flood zones must comply with the sequential approach and (where applicable) exception test. Householder development is generally exempt from the sequential test as minor development for flood risk. However, a site-specific Flood Risk Assessment is required.
Where the development increases impermeable area by 50sqm or more, a drainage strategy following the SuDS hierarchy is required.

metric.measured_from: Top of bank, or edge of culvert for culverted watercourses

_module_scope.separate_route_note: Larger home extension prior notification uses its own neighbour consultation process rather than this normal planning consultation matrix.

notes: This route is not determined under Policy A1 as a standard planning-permission assessment.
Use the larger home extension prior approval workflow instead of the full consultation matrix.

conditions_library.condition_text: The development hereby permitted shall be begun before the expiration of three years from the date of this permission.
conditions_library.reason: To comply with Section 91 of the Town and Country Planning Act 1990 as amended by Section 51 of the Planning and Compulsory Purchase Act 2004.

conditions_library.condition_text: The development hereby permitted shall be carried out in accordance with the approved plans listed in the schedule attached to this decision notice.
conditions_library.reason: To ensure that the development is carried out in accordance with the approved documents, for the avoidance of doubt.

conditions_library.condition_text: The external materials to be used in the construction of the extension hereby permitted shall match those of the existing building in type, color, and texture.
conditions_library.reason: To ensure a satisfactory appearance in the interests of visual amenity and to comply with Policy A1 of the Gloucester City Plan.

conditions_library.condition_text: The window(s) in the [specify elevation] of the extension hereby permitted serving [specify room] shall be glazed with obscure glass to Level 4 on the Pilkington scale or equivalent and shall be non-opening below 1.7m from finished floor level. The window(s) shall be permanently retained in that condition thereafter.
conditions_library.reason: To protect the privacy and amenity of the occupiers of the adjoining property in accordance with Policy SD14 of the Gloucester City Plan.

conditions_library.condition_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) Order 2015 (or any order revoking and re-enacting that Order), the parking spaces shown on the approved plans shall be retained for the parking of vehicles at all times and shall not be used for any other purpose.
conditions_library.reason: To ensure adequate parking provision is retained in the interests of highway safety and to comply with Policy INF2 of the Gloucester City Plan.

conditions_library.condition_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) Order 2015 (or any order revoking and re-enacting that Order with or without modification), no further extensions, alterations, or additions to the dwelling or outbuildings within the curtilage shall be carried out without the prior written consent of the Local Planning Authority.
conditions_library.reason: To enable the Local Planning Authority to retain control over future development in the interests of amenity and to comply with Policy A1 of the Gloucester City Plan.
conditions_library.guidance: Use sparingly - only where further PD extensions would definitely cause harm

conditions_library.condition_text: The development hereby permitted shall incorporate the flood resilience and resistance measures detailed in the approved Flood Risk Assessment [reference] to a height of 600mm above the design flood level. These measures shall be maintained in perpetuity.
conditions_library.reason: To reduce the impact of flooding on the development and its occupants in accordance with Policy E4 of the Gloucester City Plan.

conditions_library.condition_text: The development hereby permitted shall not be occupied until the surface water drainage scheme as detailed on [approved drawing reference] has been completed in accordance with the approved details. The drainage scheme shall be maintained in perpetuity.
conditions_library.reason: To ensure satisfactory drainage of the site and to reduce the risk of flooding in accordance with Policy E4 of the Gloucester City Plan.

conditions_library.condition_text: Prior to the first occupation of the extension hereby permitted, the biodiversity enhancement measures set out in the approved Biodiversity Small Sites Statement shall be implemented and thereafter maintained.
conditions_library.reason: To enhance biodiversity in accordance with Policies E1, E2 and E3 of the Gloucester City Plan.

conditions_library.condition_text: The annexe hereby permitted shall not be occupied at any time other than for purposes ancillary to the residential use of the dwelling known as [address]. The annexe shall not be severed from, sold, let or otherwise disposed of separately from the main dwelling.
conditions_library.reason: The site is not considered suitable for a separate unit of residential accommodation, and to accord with Policy A10 of the Gloucester City Plan.

informatives_library.informative_text: The applicant is advised that this planning permission does not convey any approval under the Building Regulations. Separate approval under the Building Regulations will be required for the proposed works. The applicant should contact the Building Control team at Gloucester City Council for further information.

The applicant's attention is drawn to the need to comply with all other relevant legislation, including the Party Wall etc. Act 1996, which may be applicable to this development. This is a civil matter between the parties and is separate from the planning process.

The applicant is advised that surface water from the development should be disposed of via sustainable drainage systems (SuDS) in accordance with the drainage hierarchy. Guidance is available from the Lead Local Flood Authority and in the Council's SuDS guidance note.

The applicant is encouraged to incorporate features to enhance biodiversity, such as integrated bird boxes, bat bricks, or native planting, in accordance with the Council's commitment to environmental enhancement.

This permission relates solely to the plans and documents submitted with the application as listed in the Decision Notice. Any variation from these approved plans may constitute a breach of planning control and may require a further planning application or amendment to the approved plans.

The applicant is advised that an 8 metre undeveloped buffer strip should be maintained alongside any watercourse (including culverted watercourses) on or adjacent to the site. Consent from the Environment Agency or Lead Local Flood Authority may be required for works within 8 metres of a main river or ordinary watercourse respectively.
The applicant is reminded that this planning permission does not grant listed building consent. A separate application for listed building consent will be/has been required for the proposed works.

The applicant is advised that any works to trees covered by a Tree Preservation Order or within a Conservation Area will require a separate application for consent. The applicant should contact the Council's Tree Officer before carrying out any tree works.

rates.notes: CIL rates vary by zone and are subject to indexation. Check current charging schedule.

residential_large_scale_11_to_449_dwelli.note: Subject to annual indexation - check CIL Rate Summary Statement

residential_small_scale_1_to_10_dwelling.note: Nil rated - but householder extensions over 100sqm GIA are CIL liable at the applicable rate

householder_exemption.description: Extensions and annexes under 100sqm GIA are exempt from CIL

rates.indexation: CIL payments must be index linked from 2019 to the year planning permission is granted. Annual CIL Rate Summary Statement published each December.

cil_assessment.applicability_note: Use only for planning-permission routes. Prior notification / permitted development larger home extensions are outside this CIL screening workflow.

migration_notes: Converted hybrid schema into separate facts schema, ruleset, and result schema.
Preserved narrative rule content while normalising applicability into predicate arrays where possible.
Retained unresolved TODO markers from source material; these should be managed in authoring workflow, not runtime logic.

householder_planning_permission.description: Full householder planning permission for works or extension to a dwelling.

householder_planning_permission_and_list.description: Parallel householder planning permission and listed building consent workflow.

pre_application_householder.description: Officer-style advisory review using the same merits logic where enough facts exist. Validation outputs are advisory only.

prior_notification_larger_home_extension.description: Separate larger single-storey rear extension prior notification / prior approval route.

module_purposes.local_validation_householder: Local Gloucester validation items for householder planning applications only.
module_purposes.plans_validation: Drawing and plan standards used at validation stage.
module_purposes.flood_risk_validation: Flood/drainage submission triggers at validation stage.
module_purposes.policy_A1_design_and_amenity: Planning merits assessment for design, scale, neighbour amenity and related householder issues.
module_purposes.heritage_review: Extra heritage review where listed building or conservation-area heritage content is engaged.
module_purposes.cil: Community Infrastructure Levy screening and notes.
module_purposes.prior_notification_larger_home_extension: Route-specific larger home extension prior approval workflow.

assessment_sequencing._description: Recommended processing order for MCP consumers.

steps.action: Extract facts from submission into facts schema
Run data quality checks (address consistency, lawful use confirmation, drawing specificity)
If blocking_issues exist and route is not pre_application, set planning_merits.status to not_run
Run planning merits rules where prerequisite facts exist; set cannot_assess where facts are missing
Populate recommendation based on aggregate outcomes
